Good — let’s start at the first concrete step: getting the Proxmox installer ISO and the minimal prep you need to reinstall the host as Proxmox VE with ZFS root.

1) Download the installer
- Official Proxmox downloads page (always use this page so you get the correct, signed ISO):
  https://www.proxmox.com/en/downloads
- On that page choose “Proxmox VE” → latest stable ISO (Proxmox VE 8.x as of recent releases). Click the ISO name to download, or copy the direct mirror link.

If you prefer command-line on your workstation, example (replace with the actual URL you get from the downloads page):
- wget -O proxmox-ve.iso "https://downloads.proxmox.com/iso/pve-enterprise-8.x-yyyy-mm-dd.iso"

2) Verify the ISO (checksum & signature)
- Download checksum file (md5/sha256) from the same downloads page or mirror and verify:
  - sha256sum proxmox-ve.iso
  - Compare output with the listed SHA256 on the download page.
- If a signed checksum / signature is provided, verify with GPG per Proxmox instructions.

3) Create bootable installer media (USB)

- Recommended: balenaEtcher (GUI) or dd on Linux/macOS:
  - Identify your USB device (e.g. /dev/sdX)
  - dd if=proxmox-ve.iso of=/dev/sdX bs=4M status=progress oflag=direct
  - sync
- On Windows use Rufus or balenaEtcher.

4) BIOS/firmware checklist before install
- Ensure UEFI vs Legacy mode chosen consistently (Proxmox works with UEFI or BIOS; UEFI recommended).
- Enable virtualization: AMD-V (for Ryzen 5900X) and IOMMU (AMD-Vi) if you plan to do PCI passthrough later — it’s okay to enable now.
- Disable legacy CSM if using UEFI, check secure boot is off for installer.

5) Installer choices — ZFS root (what you want)
- Boot the Proxmox installer USB and choose “Install Proxmox VE”
- On Storage/Partitioning step choose “ZFS RAID1 (mirror)” and select the two NVMe devices you have.
- Important: use ashift=12 for NVMe/4K drives (the installer usually chooses it; if it prompts for advanced options pick ashift=12).
- Set hostname, management IP, root password, and the admin email when prompted.
- Finish installer and reboot to the new Proxmox system.

6) Post-install (first actions)
- Login to the web UI at https://<your-host-ip>:8006 (use the root account).
- Add the no-subscription repo (recommended if you don’t have a Proxmox subscription):
  - echo "deb http://download.proxmox.com/debian/pve bookworm pve-no-subscription" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/pve-no-subscription.list
  - apt update && apt full-upgrade
- Configure time zone, NTP (systemd-timesyncd or chrony), hostname if needed.

7) ZFS tuning for a 5900X + 128 GB host (recommended)
- Limit ARC so it doesn’t consume all RAM (choose a value that leaves memory for VMs; with 128 GiB set ~32–48 GiB):
  - echo "options zfs zfs_arc_max=34359738368" > /etc/modprobe.d/zfs.conf   # 32 GiB
  - update-initramfs -u
  - reboot (or load via sysctl/module settings)
- Ensure compression:
  - zfs set compression=lz4 rpool

8) Storage and ISOs
- Upload your VyOS ISO and any others to Proxmox web UI: Datacenter → Storage → local (or ISO storage) → ISO Images → Upload. Or scp to /var/lib/vz/template/iso/.

- Create your ZFS-backed VM disks through the UI (it will create ZVOLs).

9) Network layout (matching your plan)
- Create bridges in Proxmox (Datacenter → node → Network):
  - vmbr0: management / public (physical NIC)
  - vmbr1: admin (system-admin VM)
  - vmbr2: prod (openclaw production/dev)
- You’ll create the VyOS router VM first and attach one NIC to vmbr0, and another to vmbr1/vmbr2 as needed.

10) VM build order
- VyOS router (edge router/firewall) — small resources
- System‑admin OpenClaw VM — for orchestration and management
- OpenClaw production/dev VM — main workload VM(s)

11) Backups
- Install Proxmox Backup Server (PBS) on a separate machine or use remote storage for offhost backups. Configure regular backup schedules and test restores.

12) If you want automation from me
I can provide exact copy/paste commands for:
- Creating an ashift=12 ZFS pool manually (if you prefer advanced install)
- Post-install tuning (ARC limit, compression)
- Creating vmbr bridges via CLI
- Creating the VyOS VM automatically (qm commands)
